title: What is React?
description: Select the correct definition of React.
options: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces
correct_answer: A JavaScript library for building user interfaces

title: What is JSX?
description: Choose the best description of JSX.
correct_answer: A syntax extension for JavaScript

title: What is a React component?
description: Select what best describes a React component.
correct_answer: A reusable piece of UI

title: What does useState return?
description: What does the useState hook return?
options: An array with the state value and a setter function
correct_answer: An array with the state value and a setter function

title: What is the virtual DOM?
description: Select the correct description of the virtual DOM.
options: A lightweight copy of the real DOM
correct_answer: A lightweight copy of the real DOM

title: What is a prop in React?
description: What are props used for in React?
options: Passing data from parent to child components
correct_answer: Passing data from parent to child components

title: What is the default export syntax?
description: Choose the correct default export syntax.
correct_answer: export default ComponentName

title: How do you create a functional component?
description: Select the correct way to create a functional component.
correct_answer: function MyComponent() { return <div />; }

title: What method renders React to the DOM?
description: Which method is used to render React elements to the DOM?
correct_answer: ReactDOM.render()

title: What is the purpose of key prop in lists?
description: Why do we use the key prop when rendering lists?
options: To help React identify which items have changed
correct_answer: To help React identify which items have changed

title: What is a fragment in React?
description: What does React.Fragment do?
options: Groups children without adding extra DOM nodes
correct_answer: Groups children without adding extra DOM nodes

title: What triggers a re-render in React?
description: Which of the following triggers a component re-render?
correct_answer: State or props change

title: What is the children prop?
description: What does props.children represent?
options: Content passed between component opening and closing tags
correct_answer: Content passed between component opening and closing tags

title: How to handle events in React?
description: What is the correct way to handle a click event?
correct_answer: onClick={handleClick}

title: What does React.createElement do?
description: Choose the correct purpose of React.createElement.
options: Creates a React element (virtual DOM node)
correct_answer: Creates a React element (virtual DOM node)

title: What is conditional rendering?
description: What does conditional rendering mean in React?
options: Rendering different UI based on conditions
correct_answer: Rendering different UI based on conditions

title: What is the StrictMode component?
description: What does React.StrictMode do?
correct_answer: Highlights potential problems in development

title: How to pass a function as a prop?
description: What is the correct way to pass a function to a child component?
correct_answer: <Child onAction={myFunction} />

title: What is a controlled component?
description: What defines a controlled component in React?
options: A component whose form data is controlled by React state
A component wrapped in a HOC
correct_answer: A component whose form data is controlled by React state

title: What is the purpose of className?
description: Why does React use className instead of class?
options: Because class is a reserved word in JavaScript
It is the same as class
correct_answer: Because class is a reserved word in JavaScript

title: What does useEffect do?
description: What is the purpose of the useEffect hook?
options: Performs side effects in function components
correct_answer: Performs side effects in function components

title: What is the initial argument to useState?
description: What does the argument passed to useState represent?
correct_answer: The initial state value

title: How to apply inline styles in React?
description: What is the correct syntax for inline styles?
correct_answer: style={{ color: 'red' }}

title: What is a single-page application?
description: What does SPA stand for in the context of React?
correct_answer: Single Page Application

title: What is the role of ReactDOM?
description: What does the ReactDOM package do?
correct_answer: Provides DOM-specific methods for rendering

title: What is a pure component?
description: What makes a component 'pure'?
options: It renders the same output for the same props and state
correct_answer: It renders the same output for the same props and state

title: What are React DevTools?
description: What is the purpose of React DevTools?
options: A browser extension for inspecting React component hierarchy
correct_answer: A browser extension for inspecting React component hierarchy

title: What is the difference between state and props?
description: Choose the correct distinction.
options: State is internal and mutable; props are external and read-only
Props are mutable; state is read-only
correct_answer: State is internal and mutable; props are external and read-only

title: What does npm start do in a React project?
description: What happens when you run npm start?
correct_answer: Starts the development server

title: What is the entry point of a React app?
description: Which file typically serves as the entry point?
correct_answer: index.js or main.jsx

title: What is Babel used for in React?
description: Choose the correct use of Babel.
options: Transpiles JSX and modern JS to browser-compatible code
correct_answer: Transpiles JSX and modern JS to browser-compatible code

title: What is the spread operator used for in React?
description: How is the spread operator commonly used?
options: To pass all props or clone objects/arrays
correct_answer: To pass all props or clone objects/arrays

title: How do you import a component?
description: Select the correct import statement.
correct_answer: import MyComponent from './MyComponent'

title: What is the purpose of render() in class components?
description: What does the render method do?
options: Returns the JSX to be rendered
correct_answer: Returns the JSX to be rendered

title: Can you use React without JSX?
description: Is JSX required for React?
options: No, you can use React.createElement instead
correct_answer: No, you can use React.createElement instead

title: What is the package.json file?
description: What does package.json do in a React project?
correct_answer: Lists project dependencies and scripts

title: What is a stateless component?
description: What defines a stateless component?
options: A component that only uses props, no internal state
correct_answer: A component that only uses props, no internal state

title: What is the useRef hook?
description: What is useRef typically used for?
options: To persist values across renders without causing re-renders
correct_answer: To persist values across renders without causing re-renders

title: How to comment inside JSX?
description: What is the correct way to add a comment in JSX?
correct_answer: {/* comment */}

title: What is Webpack in React?
description: What role does Webpack play?
options: A module bundler that bundles JS files
correct_answer: A module bundler that bundles JS files

title: What hook replaces componentDidMount?
description: Which hook is equivalent to componentDidMount?
options: useEffect with an empty dependency array
correct_answer: useEffect with an empty dependency array

title: What is the Context API?
description: What problem does React Context API solve?
options: Avoids prop drilling by sharing data across the component tree
correct_answer: Avoids prop drilling by sharing data across the component tree

title: What is useCallback used for?
description: When should you use useCallback?
options: To memoize a function so it doesn't get recreated on every render
correct_answer: To memoize a function so it doesn't get recreated on every render

title: What is useMemo?
description: What does the useMemo hook do?
options: Memoizes a computed value to avoid expensive recalculations
correct_answer: Memoizes a computed value to avoid expensive recalculations

title: What is React.memo?
description: What does wrapping a component in React.memo do?
options: Prevents re-render if props haven't changed
correct_answer: Prevents re-render if props haven't changed

title: What are Higher-Order Components?
description: What is a HOC in React?
options: A function that takes a component and returns a new enhanced component
A component with more than 10 props
correct_answer: A function that takes a component and returns a new enhanced component

title: What is the useReducer hook?
description: When would you use useReducer over useState?
options: When state logic is complex with multiple sub-values
When you have a single boolean state
correct_answer: When state logic is complex with multiple sub-values

title: What is reconciliation in React?
description: What does React's reconciliation algorithm do?
options: Diffs the virtual DOM to determine minimal real DOM updates
correct_answer: Diffs the virtual DOM to determine minimal real DOM updates

title: What is a custom hook?
description: What defines a custom hook in React?
options: A function starting with 'use' that calls other hooks
correct_answer: A function starting with 'use' that calls other hooks

title: What is the useLayoutEffect hook?
description: How does useLayoutEffect differ from useEffect?
options: It fires synchronously after all DOM mutations before paint
It only works in class components
correct_answer: It fires synchronously after all DOM mutations before paint

title: What is prop drilling?
description: What problem does prop drilling cause?
options: Passing props through many nested levels unnecessarily
Props not being passed at all
correct_answer: Passing props through many nested levels unnecessarily

title: What is lazy loading in React?
description: How do you implement lazy loading?
correct_answer: Using React.lazy() with Suspense

title: What is the Suspense component?
description: What does Suspense do in React?
options: Shows a fallback UI while waiting for lazy-loaded components
correct_answer: Shows a fallback UI while waiting for lazy-loaded components

title: What is an error boundary?
description: How do error boundaries work?
options: Class components that catch JavaScript errors in their child tree
correct_answer: Class components that catch JavaScript errors in their child tree

title: What is the difference between useEffect and useLayoutEffect?
description: Choose the key difference.
options: useLayoutEffect runs synchronously before paint; useEffect runs after
correct_answer: useLayoutEffect runs synchronously before paint; useEffect runs after

title: What is forwardRef?
description: What does React.forwardRef do?
options: Allows passing a ref through a component to a child
correct_answer: Allows passing a ref through a component to a child

title: What is the useImperativeHandle hook?
description: When is useImperativeHandle used?
options: To customize the instance value exposed when using ref with forwardRef
correct_answer: To customize the instance value exposed when using ref with forwardRef

title: What is the useContext hook?
description: What does useContext do?
options: Subscribes to a React context and returns its current value
correct_answer: Subscribes to a React context and returns its current value

title: What is code splitting?
description: What is the benefit of code splitting?
options: Loads only the code needed, reducing initial bundle size
Splits code into multiple files manually
correct_answer: Loads only the code needed, reducing initial bundle size

title: How does React handle forms?
description: What is the React way to handle form inputs?
correct_answer: Using controlled components with state

title: What is the useDebugValue hook?
description: When is useDebugValue useful?
options: To display a label in React DevTools for custom hooks
correct_answer: To display a label in React DevTools for custom hooks

title: What are portals in React?
description: What do React portals allow?
options: Rendering children into a DOM node outside the parent hierarchy
correct_answer: Rendering children into a DOM node outside the parent hierarchy

title: What is the difference between controlled and uncontrolled components?
description: Choose the correct distinction.
options: Controlled: React manages the value; Uncontrolled: DOM manages the value
correct_answer: Controlled: React manages the value; Uncontrolled: DOM manages the value

title: How to optimize a large list in React?
description: What technique helps render large lists efficiently?
correct_answer: Virtualization (e.g., react-window or react-virtualized)

title: What is the useTransition hook?
description: What does useTransition do in React 18?
options: Marks state updates as non-urgent to keep the UI responsive
correct_answer: Marks state updates as non-urgent to keep the UI responsive

title: What is the useDeferredValue hook?
description: What does useDeferredValue do?
options: Defers updating a value to allow urgent updates to render first
correct_answer: Defers updating a value to allow urgent updates to render first

title: What is batching in React 18?
description: How does automatic batching work?
options: Multiple state updates are grouped into a single re-render
Each state update triggers a separate render
Batching only works in event handlers
correct_answer: Multiple state updates are grouped into a single re-render

title: What is the purpose of useId hook?
description: What does useId do in React 18?
options: Generates unique IDs for accessibility attributes
correct_answer: Generates unique IDs for accessibility attributes

title: How does React handle synthetic events?
description: What are synthetic events?
options: Cross-browser wrapper around native DOM events
correct_answer: Cross-browser wrapper around native DOM events

title: What is the React profiler?
description: What does the Profiler component measure?
options: How often a component renders and the cost of rendering
correct_answer: How often a component renders and the cost of rendering

title: What is the difference between createElement and cloneElement?
description: Choose the correct distinction.
options: createElement creates new elements; cloneElement copies and modifies existing ones
correct_answer: createElement creates new elements; cloneElement copies and modifies existing ones

title: What is the render props pattern?
description: How does the render props pattern work?
options: A component prop that is a function returning JSX
correct_answer: A component prop that is a function returning JSX

title: How to prevent unnecessary re-renders?
description: Which technique prevents unnecessary re-renders?
correct_answer: React.memo, useMemo, and useCallback

title: What is the significance of the dependency array in useEffect?
description: What happens if you pass an empty array?
options: The effect runs only once after the initial render
The effect runs on every render
correct_answer: The effect runs only once after the initial render

title: What is React Router?
description: What does React Router provide?
correct_answer: Client-side routing for single-page applications

title: What is the Link component in React Router?
description: What does the Link component do?
correct_answer: Navigates without full page reload

title: What is the useNavigate hook?
description: What does useNavigate return?
correct_answer: A function to programmatically navigate

title: What is the useParams hook?
description: What does useParams do in React Router?
options: Returns an object of URL parameters
correct_answer: Returns an object of URL parameters

title: What is server-side rendering (SSR)?
description: What does SSR mean for React?
options: Rendering React components on the server and sending HTML to the client
correct_answer: Rendering React components on the server and sending HTML to the client

title: What is hydration in React?
description: What happens during hydration?
options: React attaches event handlers to server-rendered HTML
correct_answer: React attaches event handlers to server-rendered HTML

title: Explain React Fiber architecture.
description: What is React Fiber and why was it introduced?
options: A reimplementation of React's core algorithm enabling incremental rendering
correct_answer: A reimplementation of React's core algorithm enabling incremental rendering

title: What is concurrent rendering?
description: What does concurrent mode enable in React?
options: React can prepare multiple UI versions simultaneously without blocking the main thread
correct_answer: React can prepare multiple UI versions simultaneously without blocking the main thread

title: How does React's diffing algorithm work?
description: What is the time complexity of React's diffing?
options: O(n) using heuristics: same type = update, different type = remount
correct_answer: O(n) using heuristics: same type = update, different type = remount

title: What is the React Compiler (React Forget)?
description: What does the React Compiler aim to do?
options: Automatically memoizes components and hooks, eliminating manual useMemo/useCallback
correct_answer: Automatically memoizes components and hooks, eliminating manual useMemo/useCallback

title: What is the Scheduler in React?
description: What role does the Scheduler play?
options: Prioritizes and schedules work units based on urgency using time slicing
correct_answer: Prioritizes and schedules work units based on urgency using time slicing

title: How does server components differ from SSR?
description: What is unique about React Server Components?
options: They run exclusively on the server and send serialized output, never shipping JS to the client
They are the same as SSR
correct_answer: They run exclusively on the server and send serialized output, never shipping JS to the client

title: What are the phases of React's rendering?
description: Select the correct rendering phases.
options: Render phase (pure, can be paused) and Commit phase (applies DOM changes)
correct_answer: Render phase (pure, can be paused) and Commit phase (applies DOM changes)

title: Explain the concept of Lanes in React.
description: What are Lanes in React internals?
options: A bitmask-based priority system for categorizing and scheduling updates
correct_answer: A bitmask-based priority system for categorizing and scheduling updates

title: How does useEffect cleanup prevent memory leaks?
description: What happens when the cleanup function runs?
options: It runs before the component unmounts or before re-running the effect, canceling subscriptions/timers
correct_answer: It runs before the component unmounts or before re-running the effect, canceling subscriptions/timers

title: What is the React reconciler?
description: How does the reconciler work with the renderer?
options: The reconciler computes the diff; the renderer (e.g., ReactDOM) applies changes to the host environment
correct_answer: The reconciler computes the diff; the renderer (e.g., ReactDOM) applies changes to the host environment

title: How to implement a compound component pattern?
description: What defines the compound component pattern?
options: Parent and children share implicit state via Context, working together as a unit
correct_answer: Parent and children share implicit state via Context, working together as a unit

title: What is the tearing problem in concurrent React?
description: What causes UI tearing?
options: Reading from an external mutable store during concurrent render shows inconsistent values
correct_answer: Reading from an external mutable store during concurrent render shows inconsistent values

title: What is useSyncExternalStore?
description: What problem does useSyncExternalStore solve?
options: Safely subscribes to external stores in concurrent mode, preventing tearing
correct_answer: Safely subscribes to external stores in concurrent mode, preventing tearing

title: How does React batch updates inside promises and timeouts in React 18?
description: What changed with automatic batching?
options: React 18 batches updates everywhere including promises, timeouts, and native events
correct_answer: React 18 batches updates everywhere including promises, timeouts, and native events

title: What is the effect of key on reconciliation?
description: What happens when a key changes?
options: React destroys the old instance and creates a new one from scratch
correct_answer: React destroys the old instance and creates a new one from scratch

title: What are the trade-offs of Context vs Redux?
description: When should you prefer Redux over Context?
options: Redux for frequent updates and middleware; Context for low-frequency, theme-like data
correct_answer: Redux for frequent updates and middleware; Context for low-frequency, theme-like data

title: What is the Suspense List component?
description: What does SuspenseList do?
options: Coordinates the reveal order of multiple Suspense boundaries
correct_answer: Coordinates the reveal order of multiple Suspense boundaries

title: How does React handle priority inversion?
description: What is priority inversion in React Fiber?
options: When a high-priority update depends on a low-priority update that hasn't completed, React may replay or rebase the work
correct_answer: When a high-priority update depends on a low-priority update that hasn't completed, React may replay or rebase the work

title: Implement a custom hook for debounced search.
description: Write a custom React hook useDebounce(value, delay) that returns the debounced value. The returned value should only update after the specified delay since the last change.
correct_answer: function useDebounce(value, delay) { const [debounced, setDebounced] = useState(value); useEffect(() => { const timer = setTimeout(() => setDebounced(value), delay); return () => clearTimeout(timer); }, [value, delay]); return debounced; }

title: Implement useLocalStorage hook.
description: Create a custom hook useLocalStorage(key, initialValue) that syncs state with localStorage. It should return [storedValue, setValue] similar to useState.
correct_answer: function useLocalStorage(key, initialValue) { const [storedValue, setStoredValue] = useState(() => { const item = window.localStorage.getItem(key); return item ? JSON.parse(item) : initialValue; }); const setValue = (value) => { setStoredValue(value); window.localStorage.setItem(key, JSON.stringify(value)); }; return [storedValue, setValue]; }

title: Build a toggle component with render props.
description: Create a Toggle component that uses the render props pattern. It should manage an on/off boolean and expose toggle/on/off to children via a function.
correct_answer: function Toggle({ children }) { const [on, setOn] = useState(false); return children({ on, toggle: () => setOn(prev => !prev), setOn }); } // Usage: <Toggle>{({ on, toggle }) => <button onClick={toggle}>{on ? 'ON' : 'OFF'}</button>}</Toggle>

title: Implement an infinite scroll hook.
description: Write a useInfiniteScroll(callback) hook that triggers the callback when the user scrolls near the bottom of the page.
correct_answer: function useInfiniteScroll(callback) { useEffect(() => { const handleScroll = () => { if (window.innerHeight + document.documentElement.scrollTop >= document.documentElement.offsetHeight - 100) { callback(); } }; window.addEventListener('scroll', handleScroll); return () => window.removeEventListener('scroll', handleScroll); }, [callback]); }

title: Build a virtualized list component.
description: Implement a simple VirtualizedList component that only renders visible items based on scroll position. Props: items (array), itemHeight (number), containerHeight (number).
correct_answer:
function VirtualizedList({ items, itemHeight, containerHeight }) { const [scrollTop, setScrollTop] = useState(0); const startIndex = Math.floor(scrollTop / itemHeight); const endIndex = Math.min(startIndex + Math.ceil(containerHeight / itemHeight) + 1, items.length); const visibleItems = items.slice(startIndex, endIndex); return (
setScrollTop(e.target.scrollTop)}>
{visibleItems.map((item, i) => (
{item}
))}
); }
